summary: Iteration 7 testing: per-assignment rates, advances CRUD, payroll math, PDFs, auto-consume, reorder alerts. Backend mostly working with 2 HIGH-priority bugs: BulkAssign duplicate model strips rate_per_pair (500), MaterialIn schema missing reorder_level (silently dropped on PATCH so alerts never fire). Frontend Playwright login could not be completed via standard selectors (Sign in button uses CSS text-transform); UI checks for Payroll/Inventory/Materials not verified end-to-end through the browser, but underlying APIs they consume were exercised directly.
passed: PATCH /api/production/jobs/{id}/assignment with rate_per_pair=25 -> stored as 25 in assignments.upper.rate_per_pair
PATCH /api/production/jobs/{id}/assignment without rate_per_pair -> falls back to worker.rate_per_pair (18)
GET /api/reports/payroll math correct: Ajay(rate18)*110 = 1980 earning, second Ajay 0 earn + 500 open advance, grand net 1480 (matches spec)
GET /api/reports/payroll/{worker_id}.pdf -> 200 %PDF-1.4 (3794 bytes) for worker with data
GET /api/reports/payroll/{unknown}.pdf -> 404 with 'No payroll data for this karigar in the period'

failed.issue: POST /api/production/bulk-assign with rate_per_pair returns 500. Root cause: BulkAssign pydantic model is DEFINED TWICE in /app/backend/server.py (line 183 includes rate_per_pair, line 203 redefines without it). The 2nd definition overrides the 1st, so payload.rate_per_pair raises AttributeError at line 1118.
failed.evidence: Backend log: AttributeError: 'BulkAssign' object has no attribute 'rate_per_pair' at server.py:1118; request body {job_ids:[...], role:'upper', worker_id:'...', rate_per_pair:22} -> HTTP 500. Jobs did NOT get assignments set.

failed.issue: PATCH /api/materials/{id} with reorder_level silently drops the field. Root cause: MaterialIn model (server.py line 80) does not declare reorder_level, so payload.model_dump() omits it; subsequent GET /api/materials still has no reorder_level. As a result GET /api/inventory/alerts can never fire (always returns []) and the FRONTEND alert banner & form input can't persist their value.
failed.evidence: PATCH /api/materials/6a3b86c80bf5d7e6cea6f9e9 with reorder_level=100 -> 200 but response and subsequent GET show no reorder_level key. /api/inventory/alerts -> [] even after PATCH.

not_tested: Auto-consume inventory end-to-end: no production job exists for BOMTEST01 (only style with BOM); the 20 existing jobs are for ZFLWWFLT07274/07275 which have no BOM. Code path in _auto_consume_inventory (server.py:1470) is wired into update_job at line 871 and reads correct. PATCH stage->cutting on a no-BOM job correctly does not create movements and leaves inventory_consumed unset.

Frontend UI (Payroll KPI tiles / Advances drawer / Inventory alert banner / Materials reorder input): Playwright login could not click the 'Sign in' button via text/role selectors (rendered via CSS uppercase). Per loop-prevention I stopped after retry. Underlying backend behaviour was exercised directly via curl.
action_items: Remove the duplicate BulkAssign class at /app/backend/server.py line 203 (keep the line 183 version which includes rate_per_pair).

Add `reorder_level: Optional[float] = 0` to MaterialIn model at /app/backend/server.py line 80, OR allow the PATCH endpoint to accept reorder_level via a partial-update model. Then retest /api/inventory/alerts with a material below threshold.
Add a data-testid to the login submit button (e.g., data-testid='login-submit') so automated tests don't rely on CSS-transformed text.
Retest bulk-assign + reorder alerts + frontend Payroll/Inventory/Materials pages after fixes.
